title: Garlicky Moroccan Rice Tagine
description: Fluffy, garlicky rice infused with Moroccan spices, slow‑cooked to perfection and FODMAP‑friendly for easy meal prep.
instructions: Rinse the rice until the water runs clear, then drain.
Heat the olive oil in a heavy‑bottom pot over medium heat.
Add the garlic powder, onion powder, cumin, coriander, smoked paprika, cinnamon, turmeric, and chili flakes. Sauté for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the rice, stirring to coat each grain with the spice mixture for 2 minutes.
Pour in the vegetable broth, add salt and pepper, and bring to a gentle boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 18–20 minutes, or until the rice is tender and the liquid is absorbed.
Remove from heat and let the pot sit, covered, for 5 minutes to allow the steam to finish cooking the rice.
Fluff the rice with a fork, then stir in the chopped cilantro and lemon juice. Taste and adjust seasoning if needed.
Serve warm as a standalone dish or pair with a protein of choice, such as grilled tofu or chickpeas for a complete meal.
